title: Add delegation matrix

tasks.title: Update delegation spec/test for review+harden remediation subagents
tasks.verification: TDD red: `pnpm exec vitest run src/delegation-matrix.test.ts` failed with expected review matrix omissions (`adv-engineer`, `adv-researcher`). Green: same command passed after updating spec/test, 20 tests passed.
tasks.implementation_summary: Strengthened `plugin/src/delegation-matrix.test.ts` to require command-routed sub-agents to be represented in each delegable matrix row and added explicit review/harden remediation-substep assertions. Updated `.adv/specs/delegation-defaults/spec.json` so review allows `adv-reviewer`, `adv-engineer`, `adv-researcher`, `explore`; harden allows `adv-reviewer`, `adv-engineer`, `explore`; and remediation/research delegated substeps are structurally represented. TDD evidence: red failed on missing review `adv-engineer`/`adv-researcher`; green passed `pnpm exec vitest run src/delegation-matrix.test.ts` with 20 tests.
tasks.notes: Completed spec/test remediation alignment. Checkpoint commit f78d06d034f752a7317b61f93e883fb85cc46b99 recorded.

error_recovery.last_error: Initial formatting command ran from repo root and failed because package.json lives under plugin/. Retried from plugin/ with correct relative paths, then restored spec JSON to existing compact style to avoid broad formatting noise.
error_recovery.next_strategy: Use plugin/ as working directory for pnpm commands.

attempts.error: [ERR_PNPM_NO_PKG_MANIFEST] No package.json found in worktree root
attempts.diagnosis: Advance package commands run from plugin/, not repo root.
attempts.fix_tried: Reran prettier from plugin/ using `../.adv/...` and `src/...` paths; then reverted broad spec formatting and reapplied semantic spec changes.

tasks.title: Align review/harden command remediation routing to the matrix
tasks.verification: Updated review/harden command remediation prose to use conditional remediation routing and no ad-hoc workers. Verification passed: `pnpm exec vitest run src/delegation-matrix.test.ts src/phantom-subagent-roster.test.ts` (98 tests).
tasks.implementation_summary: Updated `.opencode/command/adv-review.md` to describe review remediation as conditional routing through `adv-reviewer`, `adv-engineer`, and `adv-researcher` rather than an independent ad-hoc choice. Updated `.opencode/command/adv-harden.md` to distinguish scanner lanes (`adv-reviewer`/`explore`) from remediation lanes (`adv-reviewer`/`adv-engineer`) and to avoid ad-hoc remediation workers. Verification passed `pnpm exec vitest run src/delegation-matrix.test.ts src/phantom-subagent-roster.test.ts` with 98 tests.
tasks.notes: Completed review/harden command remediation routing alignment. Checkpoint commit 11b5cdc8d03bca719d3a48eda459403e0c65bcb7 recorded.

tasks.title: Align ADV instruction surfaces without downstream spec lookup
tasks.verification: Aligned ADV instruction/runtime surfaces so field agents are not required to inspect repo-local delegation spec and prep pre-flight routing is removed. Verification passed: `pnpm exec vitest run src/phantom-subagent-roster.test.ts src/delegation-matrix.test.ts src/adv-instructions-assets.test.ts src/adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts` (193 tests).
tasks.implementation_summary: Updated `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md` to distinguish the Advance source-plane delegation matrix from downstream runtime guidance and to say field agents must not be required to inspect the repo-local spec. Updated `.opencode/agents/adv.md`, `SETUP.md`, and `.opencode/agents/adv-reviewer.md` to remove stale optional prep pre-flight routing and keep prep inline-only. Updated `.opencode/agents/adv.md` primary-agent roster to include `adv` and `adv-atc`. Added an `adv-reviewer` asset regression test preventing prep pre-flight routing from reappearing. Verification passed `pnpm exec vitest run src/phantom-subagent-roster.test.ts src/delegation-matrix.test.ts src/adv-instructions-assets.test.ts src/adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts` with 193 tests.

tasks.notes: Verification complete; no files changed, checkpoint recorded clean at 30de6be31506acfdd7610e7526efbb0ccf9188fe.

wisdom.content: When running package tooling in this repo, use `plugin/` as cwd; repo root has no package.json. For repo-root files like `.adv/specs/...`, pass relative paths from `plugin/` (for example `../.adv/specs/...`).

For delegation matrix drift checks, validate command files by extracting explicit Task/spawn/delegate worker targets and comparing them to each step's `allowed_subagents`; this catches hidden conditional paths such as review research/remediation workers.

Delegation defaults can drift through agent asset examples and setup docs, not just command files. When an inline-only phase changes, scan `.opencode/agents/*.md`, `ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md`, and `SETUP.md` for stale phase-specific routing such as prep pre-flight.

Phantom-subagent routing can appear as plus-separated worker prose (`explore + librarian`) in overlays and field-agent intent tables; roster tests should scan overlay surfaces and plus-routing idioms, not only spawn/delegate phrases.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved proposal with Tier A reply: continue. Proposal persisted with problem statement, scope, success criteria, constraints, failure handling, and discovery agenda.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery completed after user approved acceptance criteria with `approve`. Agreement persisted and ChangeContract minted successfully with 27 items. Discovery findings resolved field-agent boundary: matrix is an Advance repo source/evaluation artifact, not a downstream runtime lookup dependency. Retried from ADV worktree after worktree isolation block and worker restart.

design.approval_evidence: Design completed and persisted. Design uses two-plane model (Advance implementation source/evaluation vs downstream field runtime), includes D6 remediation-subagent alignment, mandatory design scout, and independent validator result CAUTION. Validator caution was resolved by adding explicit spec-law sequencing for rq-delDefaults03.4 before matrix/test updates. No user-value tradeoff, unresolved CONFLICT, or contract-compromise risk remains.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved prep plan with exact Tier A reply `approve`. Task graph contains 4 tasks with TDD intent, dependencies, and contract refs: spec/test remediation alignment; review/harden command alignment; ADV instruction/runtime alignment; focused + selected broader verification.

execution.approval_evidence: Execution complete: all 4 planned tasks are done with checkpoints. Implemented matrix/spec/test alignment, review/harden remediation command alignment, field-facing instruction cleanup, and verification. Evidence: `pnpm exec vitest run src/delegation-matrix.test.ts src/phantom-subagent-roster.test.ts src/adv-instructions-assets.test.ts src/adv-reviewer-asset.test.ts` passed (193 tests); `pnpm run check` passed. Worktree clean at 30de6be31506acfdd7610e7526efbb0ccf9188fe.

acceptance.approval_evidence: Acceptance review complete. Independent reviewer verdict: NEEDS_WORK → one non-blocking finding remediated (SETUP.md bundled-agent overview). Review matrix: 27/27 pass. 193 tests pass, pnpm run check clean. Executive summary persisted.

release.approval_evidence: Trunk merged (no-ff) and pushed. 14 files changed, reachability verified at f23f8ec.

items.text: Delegation routing defaults are structurally represented in the Advance repo rather than owned only by duplicated prompt prose.
Command and instruction assets use the matrix as an implementation/evaluation source of truth without creating a runtime lookup burden for downstream field agents.
Existing delegation safety boundaries remain enforceable by tests and review.
A `delegation-defaults` spec defines exactly 9 workflow-step matrix entries and no utility-command entries.
Each matrix entry has exactly one valid mode, one gate affinity, inline boundaries, and valid allowed sub-agent declarations.
Hybrid and subagent-primary steps structurally list delegated sub-steps and allowed sub-agents for those sub-steps.
Advance repo commands/instructions that discuss workflow-step delegation are aligned to the spec-backed matrix without requiring downstream field-agent spec lookup.
Tests fail if phantom agents or primary agents appear in sub-agent routing guidance.
Tests fail if command contracts contradict inline-required or hybrid matrix classifications.
Focused delegation verification passes from `plugin/`; broader quality checks are selected during prep and must pass before release.
Specs are laws; implementation must satisfy `.adv/specs/delegation-defaults/spec.json` or explicitly correct that spec during the change.
Implementation edits must happen in the `addDelegationMatrix` ADV worktree, not the dirty default checkout.
The change is current-repo scoped to `advance`.
Test hardening should be minimal: preserve existing matrix/roster coverage and add only what implementation changes or concrete failures require.
Command files may keep command-specific operational packets and spawn instructions when they are useful; only independent duplication of workflow-step defaults should be removed or aligned.
Task-level delegation hints may be clarified where touched, but broad runtime/schema refactors are not part of the default scope.
Do not route work to phantom or nonexistent sub-agents.
Do not route work to primary agents as sub-agents.
Do not make proposal, prep, archive, or reflect delegate work to sub-agents.
Do not require ADV agents working in downstream projects to inspect this repo-local spec during normal workflow execution.
Do not duplicate the matrix across command files or agent prompts as an independent source of truth.
Do not weaken gate ownership, human checkpoints, TDD evidence, worktree isolation, or ADV state mutation boundaries.
Utility command delegation defaults for research, tron, slop-scan, audit, improve, cleanup, clarify, status, validate, roadmap, task, idea, problem, or atc.
Runtime field-agent lookup of `.adv/specs/delegation-defaults/spec.json` in other projects.
Changing the global sub-agent nesting limit, max parallelism, or runtime Task tool guard behavior beyond matrix conformance.
Adding new sub-agents or changing existing sub-agent report schemas except where needed to prove current report-field coverage.
Broad task-routing runtime/schema refactors beyond aligning command/instruction prose with the matrix as reference.

rows.evidence: delegation-matrix.test.ts validates 9 workflow-step matrix entries; independent reviewer confirmed count
adv-review.md and adv-harden.md remediation routing aligned to spec; adv-instructions-assets.test.ts enforces alignment
ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md explicitly distinguishes source plane from runtime field plane; field agents not required to inspect repo-local spec
delegation-matrix.test.ts asserts exactly 9 entries; spec.json inspected by reviewer — 9 entries, no utility-command rows
delegation-matrix.test.ts validates mode, gate_affinity, inline_boundaries, allowed_subagents per entry
delegation-matrix.test.ts asserts delegable rows have delegated_substeps and allowed_subagents
Command files updated to route through matrix-aligned sub-agents; no field-agent spec lookup required
phantom-subagent-roster.test.ts fails on phantom or primary agents in sub-agent routing
delegation-matrix.test.ts fails if command contracts contradict inline-required or hybrid classifications
193 focused tests pass; pnpm run check (typecheck, lint, format) passes clean
Matrix lives in .adv/specs/delegation-defaults/spec.json as spec law
Two-plane model maintained: source spec + deployed runtime guidance distinct
delegation-matrix.test.ts rejects utility-command entries in matrix
No broad runtime/schema refactor performed; only alignment changes
Minimal test hardening: concrete-gap tests added for review/harden remediation; no speculative expansion
Task-level delegation_hint/delegate_preferred clarified in prose only; no schema refactor
No downstream runtime spec lookup dependency introduced
No broad task-metadata schema changes
No utility-command matrix entries added
No ad-hoc remediation workers outside matrix-declared sub-agents
No speculative test expansion beyond concrete-gap coverage
No changes to core ADV gate machinery or Temporal workflows
Runtime delegation dispatcher not touched
Plugin tool schema not changed
No new sub-agent profiles created
No CI/CD pipeline changes
No external dependency additions

acceptanceCriteria: A `delegation-defaults` spec defines exactly 9 workflow-step matrix entries and no utility-command entries.
Each matrix entry has exactly one valid mode, one gate affinity, inline boundaries, and valid allowed sub-agent declarations.
Hybrid and subagent-primary steps structurally list delegated sub-steps and allowed sub-agents for those sub-steps.
Advance repo commands/instructions that discuss workflow-step delegation are aligned to the spec-backed matrix without requiring downstream field-agent spec lookup.
Tests fail if phantom agents or primary agents appear in sub-agent routing guidance.
Tests fail if command contracts contradict inline-required or hybrid matrix classifications.
Focused delegation verification passes from `plugin/`; broader quality checks are selected during prep and must pass before release.
